skill.name: mlops-engineer
skill.description: Build MLOps pipelines, experiment tracking, model registries, deployment workflows, and monitoring plans for production machine learning systems.
skill.summary: Design production MLOps workflows across pipelines, registries, deployment, monitoring, and cloud infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings were evaluated against SKILL.md and found to be false positives. The line 26 external command alert is Markdown text for opening a resource, and the weak cryptography alerts at lines 3, 190, 203, 212, and 216 are ordinary MLOps prose, not cryptographic implementation. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Production ML systems need repeatable pipelines, governed model releases, and reliable monitoring. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design practical MLOps workflows across cloud and Kubernetes environments.
actual_capabilities: Designs ML pipeline orchestration workflows using tools such as Kubeflow, Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, and cloud-native services.
Plans experiment tracking, artifact management, model registry, and promotion workflows with tools such as MLflow, DVC, and cloud registries.
Recommends cloud MLOps architecture patterns for AWS, Azure, and GCP services.
Outlines Kubernetes, container, GitOps, and CI/CD practices for model training and deployment.
Defines monitoring, drift detection, alerting, rollback, and cost optimization strategies for production ML systems.
Includes security, compliance, governance, and audit trail considerations for ML infrastructure.
limitations: Provides architecture and workflow guidance, not direct deployment automation by itself.
Requires project-specific cloud accounts, data access, compliance needs, and existing platform constraints for precise recommendations.
The referenced implementation playbook is not present in the scanned file structure.
Generated designs should be reviewed by platform, security, and data governance owners before production use.

use_cases.title: Design a New MLOps Platform
use_cases.description: Create a cloud or Kubernetes architecture for training pipelines, model registries, deployments, monitoring, and access control.

use_cases.title: Standardize Model Release Workflows
use_cases.description: Define experiment tracking, validation gates, approval steps, and promotion paths from development to production.

use_cases.title: Improve ML Reliability Operations
use_cases.description: Plan observability, drift monitoring, rollback procedures, cost controls, and incident response for deployed models.

prompt_templates.title: Map My MLOps Needs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new ML system
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define the core MLOps requirements for a machine learning project. Ask for missing details about data, models, cloud platform, compliance, deployment target, and monitoring needs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Pipeline Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an MLOps pipeline for my project using my preferred cloud and orchestration tools. Include training, validation, registry, deployment, monitoring, rollback, and ownership responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Existing Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this MLOps architecture and identify gaps in reliability, security, governance, cost control, observability, and release management. Provide prioritized fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Cloud ML Operating Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an enterprise MLOps operating model for multiple teams and cloud platforms. Cover standards, reusable components, approval gates, compliance evidence, disaster recovery, and migration phases.

output_examples.input: Design an AWS MLOps platform for batch training and real-time inference.
output: A service architecture using storage, training jobs, model registry, CI/CD, managed endpoints, monitoring, and alerting.
A release workflow with experiment tracking, validation gates, staged deployment, rollback, and ownership checkpoints.
Operational recommendations for cost controls, access management, audit trails, and drift detection.

output_examples.input: Review our model deployment workflow for reliability risks.
output: A prioritized list of gaps in validation, deployment safety, observability, rollback, and incident response.
Concrete improvements such as canary releases, model performance alerts, data quality checks, and runbooks.
A phased remediation plan that separates quick fixes from platform-level changes.

output_examples.input: Create a governance plan for regulated machine learning workloads.
output: A governance framework covering access control, approval gates, lineage, audit evidence, and model documentation.
Recommendations for secure artifact storage, secret handling, compliance reviews, and traceable model promotion.
A checklist for production readiness and ongoing operational review.

best_practices: Start with clear ownership, model release criteria, rollback rules, and monitoring responsibilities before choosing tools.
Use versioned data, code, parameters, artifacts, and environments so experiments and deployments remain reproducible.
Design observability, access control, cost tracking, and compliance evidence as first-class platform requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not promote models to production without validation gates, approvals, rollback plans, and monitoring coverage.
Do not treat notebooks, ad hoc scripts, or manual cloud console steps as the long-term deployment process.
Do not select orchestration tools before defining workload scale, team skills, compliance needs, and failure modes.

faq.question: What kind of MLOps work does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports architecture planning, pipeline design, model registry workflows, deployment strategy, monitoring, governance, and cloud MLOps recommendations.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready infrastructure code?
faq.answer: It can help plan infrastructure and implementation steps, but generated code still needs review, testing, and environment-specific changes.

faq.question: Which platforms and tools are covered?
faq.answer: The skill references MLflow, Kubeflow, Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, DVC, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, GCP, and common monitoring tools.

faq.question: Is this useful for regulated ML systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes security, compliance, governance, audit trail, access control, and model approval considerations.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or connect to cloud accounts?
faq.answer: No. The scanned skill file provides guidance only and does not contain executable scripts or cloud account access logic.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide the cloud platform, data sources, model types, deployment targets, compliance needs, team ownership, scale, and current tooling.
